topic: AI Coding Tools for Mobile Developers: iOS & Android Workflows in 2026

competitors.title: Gemma 4 Edge Deployment: Mobile (Android/iOS) and IoT On-Device AI Guide
key_points: Gemma 4 E2B/E4B deployment on Android via MediaPipe + LiteRT stack
iOS deployment with Core ML and MLX Swift frameworks
Memory footprint optimization for mobile devices (E2B: 2.2GB, E4B: 4.5GB)
Android Studio now supports Gemma 4 as local model for agentic coding (announced April 2026)

competitors.title: Cursor vs Windsurf vs Antigravity: AI IDE Comparison for Mobile Development
key_points: All VS Code-based AI IDEs support mobile development workflows
Cursor has mature agentic features (2+ years) with dedicated mobile project templates
Antigravity offers unique parallel agent orchestration for cross-platform testing
Native Chrome integration in Antigravity valuable for mobile web app testing

competitors.title: Windsurf vs Cursor 2026: AI IDE Features for Mobile Development
key_points: Windsurf Arena Mode supports blind model comparison for mobile-specific tasks
Cursor's SWE-1.5 at 950 tok/s optimized for agentic mobile development tasks
Multi-agent sessions with Git worktrees support iOS/Android simultaneous development
Codebase understanding (Codemaps) valuable for complex mobile codebases

competitors.title: Free Run Google's Gemma 4 Locally: Ollama Setup for Mobile Developers
key_points: Beginner-friendly Ollama setup for local AI coding assistance
Single-command install works across iOS/macOS and Android/Linux
Free tier/no-cost local inference ideal for indie mobile developers
Quantization options for mobile hardware constraints

stats.fact: 85% of mobile developers use at least one AI tool in their workflow (Index.dev AI Pair Programming Stats 2026)
Android Studio supports Gemma 4 as the local model for agentic coding (announced April 2026)
Cursor holds SOC 2 Type II certification while Antigravity has documented critical security vulnerabilities for mobile projects
22% of merged mobile app code is AI-authored across 135,000+ developers
Mobile developers using AI tools merge ~60% more pull requests than light/non-users
Gemma 4 memory footprint: E2B (2.2GB), E4B (4.5GB) - optimized for modern mobile devices
Windsurf acquired by Cognition (Devin AI) in July 2025 for $250M focusing on mobile AI development
Antigravity Manager View runs up to 5 parallel agents - ideal for iOS and Android simultaneous testing

angles: iOS vs Android AI tool specialization: Different ecosystems require different AI approaches

Mobile-specific testing automation: AI agents for UI testing, performance profiling, and crash analysis
Indie vs enterprise mobile AI: Cost considerations for solo developers vs teams
Future of mobile AI coding: Gemini Nano integration, Apple/Google platform-specific AI features
